subject: Math
domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

lesson.story_mission: Join the Base-Ten Beacon team to decode numbers, round them to friendly landmarks, and solve place-value addition and subtraction clues.
lesson.mini_lesson: Place value tells the value of each digit. To round, find the nearest ten or hundred by checking the midpoint.
lesson.objective: Use place value to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 and add or subtract with place-value strategies.
lesson.concept_explanation: A digit has a value based on its place. Rounding replaces a number with a nearby ten or hundred. If the number is at the midpoint or higher, round up.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may round the wrong direction, treat midpoint numbers as rounding down, misread a digit place, or ignore zero placeholders.
lesson.real_world_connection: Rounding helps estimate distances, supplies, scores, and totals quickly.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read each place, use expanded form, compare to the midpoint, and round to the requested place.

worked_examples.prompt: In 246, what digit is in the hundreds place?
hints: Look at the place named in the question.
Use the digit to the right to decide if rounding is needed.
Check that your answer matches the requested place.

game_theme.title: Base-Ten Beacon
game_theme.name: Base-Ten Beacon
game_theme.mission_intro: Light the beacon by naming digit values and rounding numbers to nearby landmarks.
